user.title: WAHA (WhatsApp HTTP API)
user.description: Connect to your self-hosted WAHA server. After saving, point WAHA's webhook at the URL shown on the integration's overview page.

data_description.base_url: Example: http://localhost:3000 — no trailing slash needed.
data_description.api_key: Required if your WAHA instance has WHATSAPP_API_KEY set.
data_description.session: Defaults to 'default'. Must match the session you started in WAHA.

abort.already_configured: This WAHA base URL + session is already configured.

init.title: WAHA — inbound message options
init.description: Control how incoming WhatsApp messages are handled. Every message always fires the `waha_message_received` event, regardless of these settings.

data.forward_to_conversation: Forward incoming messages to a conversation agent
data.reply_with_agent_response: Reply to the sender with the agent's response
data.allowed_senders: Allowed senders (comma-separated chat IDs; blank = allow all)

data_description.conversation_agent: Find this in Settings → Voice assistants. Example: conversation.openai or leave blank for the default Assist pipeline.

send_message.name: Send WhatsApp message
send_message.description: Send a WhatsApp text message via WAHA.
